introduction.text: For project {project_id}, conduct a comprehensive FSI risk assessment following the FSI Risk Framework Template structure. Follow this exact workflow:

step.title: AGENT DISCOVERY (REQUIRED FIRST STEP)
step.description: ALWAYS perform agent discovery BEFORE starting any assessment workflow:

requirements.requirement: Use the built-in agent discovery tool to identify available agents
Verify connectivity to Architect Agent, Security Architect Agent, and Auditor Agent
Only proceed with assessment after confirming agent availability

abort_conditions.condition: If Architect Agent is NOT available - ABORT assessment immediately
If Security Architect Agent is NOT available - ABORT assessment immediately
abort_conditions.abort_message: Cannot proceed with risk assessment. Required agents (Architect and/or Security Architect) are not available. Please ensure all agents are running before attempting risk assessment.

step.title: READ ORGANIZATION PROFILE
step.description: Read the organization profile to understand the context for this assessment:

requirements.requirement: Call get_org_profile tool with the project_id to retrieve the linked organization profile
Note the organization's: industry, size, jurisdictions, compliance frameworks, risk appetite, crown jewels, security maturity, threat landscape, and key vendors
Use this context throughout the assessment to calibrate risk scores, identify relevant frameworks, and prioritize findings based on crown jewels and business processes

step.note: If no profile is linked, proceed with generic FSI assumptions but note this limitation in the report.

step.title: GATHER INFORMATION
step.description: After confirming agent availability, use your A2A communication tools to gather required information:

message.title: Message to Architect Agent:
message.requirement: Use only actual Architecture Agent findings
message.content: For project {project_id}, perform a fresh architecture assessment. Analyze the architecture diagram, identify all components, data flows, and architecture issues. Return the full review content to me directly. Do not ask follow-up questions.

message.title: Message to Security Architect Agent:
message.requirement: Use only actual Security Agent findings
message.content: For project {project_id}, perform a fresh security assessment. Identify all security issues, vulnerabilities, control gaps, and compliance concerns. Return the full assessment content to me directly. Do not ask follow-up questions.

message.title: Message to Auditor Agent (use invoke_agent tool):
message.content: For project {project_id}, perform a final audit of the completed risk assessment. Review the assessment for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with FSI framework requirements. Provide audit findings and recommendations.

step.title: EVIDENCE VALIDATION (MANDATORY)
step.description: Before proceeding to Step 2, you MUST validate the evidence collected:

validation_requirements.requirement: ✅ Confirm Architecture Agent provided actual findings (not just "no issues")
✅ Confirm Security Architect Agent provided specific vulnerabilities/gaps
✅ Validate evidence completeness before risk scoring
✅ Apply risk calibration based on findings severity

risk_calibration_matrix.calibration: Low-Medium risk profile (0-1 High risks maximum)
Medium risk profile (1-2 High risks maximum)

evidence_alignment_requirements.requirement: Each HIGH/CRITICAL risk MUST cite specific Architecture/Security findings
Risk scenarios MUST match actual project conditions (not theoretical)
Business impact MUST align with demonstrated gaps

step.title: CONDUCT FSI RISK ASSESSMENT
step.description: Using the information gathered above, create a comprehensive FSI risk assessment report following these requirements:

critical_requirements.requirement: generate risk assessments based actual evidence from our technical assessment agents
CONDUCT ACTUAL FSI RISK ANALYSIS - analyze the gathered data and create comprehensive risk assessment
Map all identified risks to the 8 FSI business risk categories
Calculate residual risk ratings and tolerance levels
Include specific risk scenarios, mitigation strategies, and recommendations
NEVER just save empty templates - always perform actual risk analysis first

SEPARATE risks into two categories in the report: 1. SOLUTION-INTRODUCED RISKS — new risks created by THIS architecture/design (e.g., missing WAF, static credentials in new services, no DLQ). These are what the project team must fix. 2. INHERITED ENTERPRISE RISKS — pre-existing organizational risks from the org profile that affect this solution (e.g., existing audit findings, known gaps in IR drills, service account rotation backlog). These are context for scoring but owned by the enterprise, not this project. Present them in separate sections in the summary and clearly label each risk with [SOLUTION] or [ENTERPRISE] in the risk table.

FACTUAL REMEDIATION TIMELINES — do NOT invent arbitrary timelines (e.g., "0-30 days"). Instead: 1. Use the organization's patch SLA from the org profile to set severity-based timelines (e.g., if org says critical=4hrs, high=30 days, use those) 2. For risks that overlap with existing audit findings, reference the existing due dates from the org profile 3. Use effort ratings from the security/architecture assessment (LOW=days, MEDIUM=weeks, HIGH=months) calibrated against the org's change velocity (deploys/week) 4. Reference the org's change velocity to estimate realistic deployment timelines (e.g., "35-45 deploys/week means LOW effort fixes can ship within 1 sprint") 5. If a timeline cannot be grounded in org data, state "Timeline TBD — requires project team estimation" rather than guessing

risk_count_consistency.rule: Executive Summary total MUST equal detailed scenarios count
Executive Summary total MUST equal risk matrix rows
Detailed scenarios count MUST equal risk matrix rows
risk_count_consistency.example: If you identify 9 risks, you MUST have 9 detailed scenarios AND 9 matrix rows

complete_risk_scenarios.description: Every identified risk MUST include ALL of these elements:

elements.element: Risk Categories - Primary and secondary FSI categories
Technical Evidence - Exactly ONE paragraph with specific technical findings
Risk Assessment Matrix - Visual 5x5 matrix with highlighted cell
Business Impact Analysis - Likelihood, impact, financial/operational/regulatory impacts
Risk Management Strategy - Controls, effectiveness, actions, owner, timeline

business_language_requirements.executive_sections: Use business terms ("revenue loss", "operational disruption", "regulatory fines")
business_language_requirements.quantify_impact: Include dollar amounts, timeframes, customer numbers
business_language_requirements.strategic_focus: Competitive advantage, market position, stakeholder confidence

technical_evidence_requirements.description: For each risk scenario, include exactly one paragraph:

format.text: [One paragraph with specific technical findings, architecture gaps, security vulnerabilities, or compliance deficiencies that support this business risk. Include component names, configuration issues, or missing controls.]

template_reference.text: Follow the FSI Risk Framework Template structure below:

step.title: AUDIT REVIEW
step.description: After completing and saving the risk assessment, automatically send the final report to the Auditor Agent for review using your A2A communication tools:

mandatory_action.message: Please audit this completed FSI risk assessment for project {project_id}. Retrieve findings from Architect Agent and Security Architect Agent and cross reference with risks, only allow risks which can cross referenced and are evidence based. Review for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with FSI framework standards. The assessment has been saved and is ready for your final review.

step.post_audit_action: After receiving the Auditor's response, append an "Audit Summary" section to the end of your assessment output that includes: - Auditor verdict (APPROVED / CONDITIONAL / REJECTED) - Key findings from the audit - Any risks that were flagged as lacking evidence or traceability - Validation status of risk categorization, timelines, and framework citations Present this to the user as part of the final assessment output.

output_format_requirements.critical_note: You MUST generate the report in MARKDOWN format using the EXACT template structure above. DO NOT generate HTML, DO NOT create your own format.

mandatory_requirements.requirement: Use EXACT template structure - Follow every section, heading, and format from the FSI Risk Framework Template above
Generate MARKDOWN only - No HTML, no custom styling, pure markdown
Replace ALL placeholders - Fill in [PROJECT_NAME], [CURRENT_DATE], [RISK_NAME], etc. with actual data
Include ALL sections - Every section from Table of Contents through Recommendations must be present
Follow exact numbering - Use the same section numbers (1. Business Risk Categories, 2. Risk Matrix, etc.)
Use template tables - Keep the exact table structures for likelihood, impact, risk matrix, etc.
Fill risk scenarios - Replace example scenarios with actual project-specific risks
Include visual risk matrices - For each risk scenario, include the HTML-styled 5x5 risk matrix table with color coding

risk_calibration_framework.minimal_findings: Low-Medium risk profile (0-1 High risks maximum)
risk_calibration_framework.moderate_findings: Medium risk profile (1-2 High risks maximum)

risk_matrix_colors.note: Bold border and highlight the specific cell where the risk falls based on its likelihood and impact ratings

template_compliance_check.check: ✅ Starts with "# FSI Risk Assessment Report"
✅ Has Table of Contents with exact links
✅ Ends with footer format from template

critical_action.description: After generating the complete assessment following the EXACT template format, you MUST call save_risk_assessment with these exact parameters:

parameters.parameter: [the complete FSI risk assessment report you just generated in EXACT template format]

critical_action.final_step: Then present the complete FSI Risk Assessment Report to the user for review.
